Tutorial 13 — Advanced Features and Customisation¶

Prerequisites: Tutorials 01–12

This final tutorial covers the remaining public API surface: extension points for non-standard biology, convenience tools for model introspection, and features that fit most naturally after understanding the full model.

What you will learn

  1. allow_superinfection — phage adsorption to already-infected bacteria
  2. Dynamic phenotypic switching (with_transition_function)
  3. Custom lysis-progression function (with_lysis_function)
  4. Growth/dormancy presets (CONSTANT, LOGISTIC, MONOD, MONOD_LOGISTIC)
  5. show_equations — LaTeX ODE display
  6. get_parameters — runtime parameter introspection
  7. track_nutrients — freezing S for non-Monod models (recap)
  8. imm_kill_rate_D — immune killing of dormant bacteria (recap)
  9. OD measurement — debris ODE (with_od_debris) and od_to_cfu_conversion_factor
  10. Dormant initial conditions — dorm_frac and InitialConditions.D
  11. Stationary-phase initial conditions — stationary_phase_ic() (now returns Imm)
  12. Nutrient inflow and washout — s_in and s_out
  13. Extinction threshold — absorbing barrier for the atto-fox problem (extinction_threshold in solve_ode)
  14. Immune CR/CS — per-genotype immune kill and stimulation rates (cr_imm_kill_rate, cr_imm_stim_rate)

1 Imports¶

In [1]:
import warnings

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from pbisim import (ModelBuilder, PBIModel, solve_ode,
                    CONSTANT, LOGISTIC, MONOD, MONOD_LOGISTIC, ALL_PRESETS,
                    apply_preset, show_equations, get_parameters,
                    DoseEvent, DoseSchedule,
                    InitialConditions, stationary_phase_ic)
from pbisim.strains import BinaryResistanceGenotypes, BacterialStrain, PhageStrain

2 Superinfection — phage adsorption to infected bacteria¶

By default, phage only adsorb to active uninfected bacteria (B) and dormant uninfected bacteria (D). Once a bacterium is infected (I or H), it is shielded from secondary phage infection — a simplification based on the concept of superinfection exclusion (where the first infecting phage blocks subsequent ones).

Setting allow_superinfection=True removes this shield: phage can also adsorb to I and H compartments. This is biologically relevant when:

  • Modelling phage cocktails where genetic recombination between co-infecting phages generates novel variants (directed evolution)
  • Studying lytic–lysogenic switching where double infection affects fate
  • Benchmarking against experimental data that shows phage loss beyond what B + D adsorption alone can explain

The secondary adsorption depletes the free-phage pool but does NOT create a new infected compartment (host fate is unchanged for simplicity).

In [2]:
# Two-strain model: strain 0 is phage-susceptible (adsorption=1e-8),
# strain 1 is phage-resistant (adsorption=0).  With superinfection enabled,
# phage also adsorb to already-infected I/H bacteria, depleting the phage
# pool faster — especially visible when many bacteria are in the infected state.
base_si = (
    ModelBuilder(n_bacteria=2, n_phages=1, n_latent=5)
    .with_growth_rates([1.2, 1.15], bacteria_to_resource_ratio=1e9)
    .with_phage_params(adsorption_rates=[1e-8, 0], burst_sizes=20.0,
                       latent_periods=2, phage_decay_rates=0.05)
    .with_nutrient(monod_constant=0.3, recycle_fraction=0.0)
    .with_dose_schedule(DoseSchedule([
        DoseEvent(time=0, amount=5e6, target="phage", index=0)
    ]))
)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))
for (si, color, label) in [
    (False, "C0", "Superinfection excluded (default)"),
    (True,  "C2", "allow_superinfection=True"),
]:
    cfg = base_si.with_phage_params(allow_superinfection=si).build()
    res = solve_ode(
        PBIModel(cfg, initial_B=np.array([1e7, 1e3]),
                 initial_P=np.array([0.0]), initial_S=1.0),
        t_end=48.0, dt=0.25,
    )
    axes[0].semilogy(res.time,
                     np.maximum(res.sum_prefixes("B","D","I","H"), 1),
                     color=color, label=label)
    axes[1].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(res.get("P0"), 1),
                     color=color, label=label)

axes[0].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL", title="Total bacteria")
axes[1].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="PFU/mL",
            title="Free phage — superinfection depletes phage faster")
for ax in axes: ax.legend(fontsize=9)
plt.suptitle("Superinfection: phage also adsorb to infected (I, H) bacteria")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

3 Dynamic transition function — phage-pressure-induced resistance switching¶

with_transition_function(fn) adds a state-dependent phenotypic switch on top of the static transition_rates matrix. The function is evaluated at every ODE time step and its output is ADDED to the static matrix.

Biological application: phage stress triggers upregulation of resistance mechanisms in some bacteria (e.g., CRISPR adaptation, OM protein downregulation). When phage density is high, susceptible bacteria switch faster to a resistant phenotype.

In [3]:
def phage_driven_switch(S, B, P, config):
    # Phage-pressure-dependent rate of switching susceptible -> resistant
    n = config.n_bacteria
    m = config.n_phages
    phage_pressure = float(np.sum(P)) / (1e6 + float(np.sum(P)))  # saturating signal
    k_switch = 0.05 * phage_pressure   # max switching rate 0.05 h^-1
    T = np.zeros((n, n))
    if n >= 2:
        T[1, 0] =  k_switch   # S -> R rate increases with phage
        T[0, 0] = -k_switch   # S loses bacteria at same rate
    return T

cfg_switch = (
    ModelBuilder(n_bacteria=2, n_phages=1, n_latent=5)
    .with_growth_rates([1.0, 0.92])
    .with_phage_params(
        adsorption_rates=np.array([[2e-9], [2e-11]]),
        adsorption_rates_dormant=np.array([[2e-11], [2e-13]]),
        burst_sizes=50.0, latent_periods=0.5, phage_decay_rates=0.1,
    )
    .with_transition_function(phage_driven_switch)  # dynamic switch
    .with_nutrient(monod_constant=0.3, recycle_fraction=0.0)
    .with_dose_schedule(DoseSchedule([
        DoseEvent(time=0, amount=5e8, target="phage", index=0)
    ]))
    .build()
)

res_switch = solve_ode(
    PBIModel(cfg_switch, initial_B=np.array([5e7, 1e3]),
             initial_P=np.array([0.0]), initial_S=1.0),
    t_end=72.0, dt=0.25,
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
for i, (label, color) in enumerate([("Susceptible (S)", "C0"),
                                     ("Resistant (R)",   "C2")]):
    ax.semilogy(res_switch.time, np.maximum(res_switch.get(f"B{i}"), 1),
                color=color, label=label)
ax.set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL",
       title="Phage-pressure-driven phenotypic switching S→R")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

4 Growth/dormancy presets¶

Presets bundle a consistent set of growth, dormancy, and resuscitation functions so you do not need to manually verify their compatibility.

Preset Growth Dormancy trigger track_nutrients
CONSTANT Constant (no limit) Constant rate False
LOGISTIC Logistic (density) Density-dependent False
MONOD Monod (nutrient) Nutrient-dependent True
MONOD_LOGISTIC Monod × logistic Nutrient + density True

apply_preset(cfg, preset) returns a new ModelConfig with the preset's functions applied. It also sets track_nutrients correctly.

In [4]:
presets = [CONSTANT, LOGISTIC, MONOD, MONOD_LOGISTIC]
labels  = ["CONSTANT", "LOGISTIC", "MONOD", "MONOD_LOGISTIC"]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))

for preset, label, color in zip(presets, labels, ["C0","C1","C2","C3"]):
    # Start from a minimal config
    kw = {"carrying_capacity": 1e9} if label in ("LOGISTIC","MONOD_LOGISTIC") else {}
    cfg_p = (
        ModelBuilder(n_bacteria=1, n_phages=0)
        .with_growth_rates(1.0, bacteria_to_resource_ratio=1e9)
        .with_dormancy(dormancy_rate=0.1, resuscitation_rate=0.05)
        .with_nutrient(monod_constant=0.3, **kw,
                       track_nutrients=preset in (MONOD, MONOD_LOGISTIC))
        .build()
    )
    cfg_p = apply_preset(cfg_p, preset)
    res_p = solve_ode(
        PBIModel(cfg_p, initial_B=np.array([1e4]),
                 initial_P=np.zeros(0), initial_S=1.0),
        t_end=24.0, dt=0.1,
    )
    ax.semilogy(res_p.time, np.maximum(res_p.get("B0"), 1),
                color=color, label=label)

ax.set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL",
       title="Preset growth functions — all four built-in presets")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

5 show_equations and get_parameters — model introspection¶

Two tools help you verify that the model you built is what you intended:

  • model.show_equations(): renders the full ODE system as LaTeX in Jupyter, or prints raw LaTeX in a terminal. Flags which features are active (phage PK, dormancy, antibiotic PD, etc.).

  • model.get_parameters(): returns all current parameter values as a flat dictionary — useful for sanity-checking that broadcasting and defaults worked correctly before a long simulation run.

In [5]:
# Build a model with several features active
cfg_inspect = (
    ModelBuilder(n_bacteria=2, n_phages=1, n_latent=3, n_depth=2)
    .with_growth_rates([1.0, 0.93], bacteria_to_resource_ratio=1e9)
    .with_phage_params(adsorption_rates=np.array([[2e-9],[2e-11]]),
                       burst_sizes=50.0, latent_periods=0.5,
                       phage_decay_rates=0.1)
    .with_dormancy(dormancy_rate=0.1, resuscitation_rate=0.05,
                   dormancy_signal="nutrient", resuscitation_signal="nutrient")
    .with_immunity(immune_module="innate", imm_stim_rate=1.0,
                   imm_kill_rate=3e6, imm_decay_rate=0.15,
                   imm_stim50=5e6, imm_kill50=5e6)
    .with_mutations(phage_resistance_rates=[5e-8])
    .with_nutrient(monod_constant=0.3, recycle_fraction=0.3)
    .build()
)
model_inspect = PBIModel(cfg_inspect,
                          initial_B=np.array([5e7, 1e2]),
                          initial_P=np.array([0.0]), initial_S=1.0)

# Print a subset of parameters (full dict is long)
params = model_inspect.get_parameters()
keys_to_show = ["growth_rates", "adsorption_rates", "burst_sizes",
                "mutation_rates", "dormancy_rate", "imm_kill_rate",
                "recycle_fraction"]
print("Key model parameters:")
for k in keys_to_show:
    print(f"  {k}: {params[k]}")

# In Jupyter this renders LaTeX; in terminal it prints the source
print("\nODE system (plain text — renders as LaTeX in Jupyter):")
model_inspect.show_equations(plain=True)

Key model parameters:
  growth_rates: [1.   0.93]
  adsorption_rates: [[2.e-09]
 [2.e-11]]
  burst_sizes: [[50.]
 [50.]]
  mutation_rates: [[-5.e-08  0.e+00]
 [ 5.e-08  0.e+00]]
  dormancy_rate: [0.1 0.1]
  imm_kill_rate: [3000000. 3000000.]
  recycle_fraction: 0.3

ODE system (plain text — renders as LaTeX in Jupyter):
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PBIModel — 2 strains, 1 phage
Active: Monod growth, dormancy (Q=2), innate immunity
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ODE System (LaTeX source):

\begin{aligned}
  \dfrac{dB_i}{dt} &= \mu_i\,f(S)\,B_i && \text{\small growth} \\
    &\quad +\; \textstyle\sum_k M_{ik}\,B_k && \text{\small mutations from/to other strains} \\
    &\quad -\; \varphi_i\,B_i && \text{\small entry into dormancy} \\
    &\quad +\; \rho_i\,D_{0,i} && \text{\small resuscitation} \\
    &\quad -\; \textstyle\sum_j \beta_{ij}\,P_j\,B_i && \text{\small phage adsorption} \\
    &\quad -\; \frac{\kappa_i\,\mathrm{Imm}\,B_i}{K_\mathrm{kill}+B_\mathrm{tot}} && \text{\small immune killing} \\\\
  \dfrac{dP_j}{dt} &= \textstyle\sum_i N_{ij}\,\gamma_{ij}\,I_{3,i,j}\,\phi && \text{\small burst lysis from active infected (last latent stage)} \\
    &\quad +\; \textstyle\sum_{d,i} N_{ij}\,\gamma_{ij}^D\,H_{d,3,i,j}\,\phi && \text{\small burst lysis from hibernating bacteria} \\
    &\quad -\; \textstyle\sum_i \beta_{ij}\,B_i\,P_j && \text{\small adsorption to active bacteria} \\
    &\quad -\; \textstyle\sum_{d,i}\beta_{ij}^D e^{-\alpha_{ij}d}\,D_{d,i}\,P_j && \text{\small adsorption to dormant bacteria} \\
    &\quad -\; \delta_j\,P_j && \text{\small phage decay} \\\\
  \dfrac{dI_{l,i,j}}{dt} &= \beta_{ij}\,B_i\,P_j\;\mathbf{1}_{[l=1]} && \text{\small new infection (first stage only)} \\
    &\quad -\; \gamma_{ij}\,I_{l,i,j}\,\phi && \text{\small progression / lysis at last stage} \\
    &\quad +\; \gamma_{ij}\,I_{l-1,i,j}\,\phi\;\mathbf{1}_{[l>1]} && \text{\small arrival from previous stage} \\
    &\quad -\; \varphi_i\,I_{l,i,j} && \text{\small dormancy entry} \\
    &\quad +\; \rho_i\,H_{0,l,i,j} && \text{\small resuscitation from hibernation} \\
    &\quad -\; \frac{\kappa_i\,\mathrm{Imm}\,I_{l,i,j}}{K_\mathrm{kill}+B_\mathrm{tot}} && \text{\small immune killing} \\\\
  \dfrac{dD_{d,i}}{dt} &= \varphi_i\,B_i\;\mathbf{1}_{[d=1]} && \text{\small dormancy entry (surface layer)} \\
    &\quad -\; \rho_i\,D_{0,i}\;\mathbf{1}_{[d=1]} && \text{\small resuscitation (surface layer only)} \\
    &\quad -\; \xi_i\,(1-f_S)\,D_{d,i} && \text{\small diffusion to deeper layer} \\
    &\quad +\; \xi_i\,f_S\,D_{d+1,i}\;\mathbf{1}_{[d<Q]} && \text{\small diffusion toward surface} \\
    &\quad -\; \textstyle\sum_j\beta_{ij}^D e^{-\alpha_{ij}d}\,D_{d,i}\,P_j && \text{\small phage infection of dormant bacteria} \\\\
  \dfrac{d\mathrm{Imm}}{dt} &= \frac{\textstyle\sum_i \sigma_i\bigl(B_i + \textstyle\sum_{l,j}I_{l,i,j}\bigr)}{K_\mathrm{stim}+B_\mathrm{tot}} && \text{\small immune stimulation} \\
    &\quad -\; \delta_\mathrm{Imm}\,\mathrm{Imm} && \text{\small immune decay} \\\\
  \dfrac{dS}{dt} &= \textstyle\sum_i\frac{\mu_i f(S)\,B_i}{Y_i} && \text{\small nutrient consumption by growing bacteria} \\
    &\quad +\; \eta\,\textstyle\sum_i\frac{\text{deaths}_i}{Y_i} && \text{\small biomass recycling from all death events}
\end{aligned}

State variables:
  B_i                             active bacteria, strain i = 1 … 2
  P_j                             free phage, species j = 1 … 1
  I_{l,i,j}                       active infected, latent stage l = 1 … 3
  D_{d,i}                         dormant bacteria, depth d = 1 … 2
  H_{d,l,i,j}                     dormant + infected (hibernating)
  \mathrm{Imm}                    immune effector concentration
  S                               nutrient substrate

Parameters:
  \mu_i                           maximum growth rate (h⁻¹)
  f(S)                            growth signal ∈ [0, 1]
  Y_i                             bacteria-to-resource ratio
  K_s                             Monod half-sat. = 0.3
  M_{ik}                          mutation rate from strain k → i per replication
  \beta_{ij}                      adsorption rate of phage j to strain i (mL h⁻¹)
  N_{ij}                          burst size — phage released per lysis event
  \gamma_{ij}                     latent-stage progression rate = (L+1) / τ_{ij}
  \tau_{ij}                       mean latent period of active infected (h)
  \delta_j                        phage decay / clearance rate (h⁻¹)
  \phi                            lysis progression signal (= f(S) by default)
  \varphi_i                       dormancy entry rate (h⁻¹)
  \rho_i                          resuscitation rate (h⁻¹)
  \xi_i                           dormancy-depth diffusion rate constant (h⁻¹)
  f_S                             nutrient signal for depth diffusion = S / (K_s + S)
  \beta_{ij}^D                    adsorption rate to dormant bacteria
  \alpha_{ij}                     depth attenuation of phage penetration
  \sigma_i                        immune stimulation rate per bacterium
  \kappa_i                        immune kill rate coefficient
  K_\mathrm{stim}                 immune stimulation half-sat. = 5e+06
  K_\mathrm{kill}                 immune killing half-sat. = 5e+06
  \delta_\mathrm{{Imm}}           immune effector decay rate = 0.15
  \eta                            biomass recycle fraction = 0.3
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Out[5]:
'\\begin{aligned}\n  \\dfrac{dB_i}{dt} &= \\mu_i\\,f(S)\\,B_i && \\text{\\small growth} \\\\\n    &\\quad +\\; \\textstyle\\sum_k M_{ik}\\,B_k && \\text{\\small mutations from/to other strains} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\varphi_i\\,B_i && \\text{\\small entry into dormancy} \\\\\n    &\\quad +\\; \\rho_i\\,D_{0,i} && \\text{\\small resuscitation} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\textstyle\\sum_j \\beta_{ij}\\,P_j\\,B_i && \\text{\\small phage adsorption} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\frac{\\kappa_i\\,\\mathrm{Imm}\\,B_i}{K_\\mathrm{kill}+B_\\mathrm{tot}} && \\text{\\small immune killing} \\\\\\\\\n  \\dfrac{dP_j}{dt} &= \\textstyle\\sum_i N_{ij}\\,\\gamma_{ij}\\,I_{3,i,j}\\,\\phi && \\text{\\small burst lysis from active infected (last latent stage)} \\\\\n    &\\quad +\\; \\textstyle\\sum_{d,i} N_{ij}\\,\\gamma_{ij}^D\\,H_{d,3,i,j}\\,\\phi && \\text{\\small burst lysis from hibernating bacteria} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\textstyle\\sum_i \\beta_{ij}\\,B_i\\,P_j && \\text{\\small adsorption to active bacteria} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\textstyle\\sum_{d,i}\\beta_{ij}^D e^{-\\alpha_{ij}d}\\,D_{d,i}\\,P_j && \\text{\\small adsorption to dormant bacteria} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\delta_j\\,P_j && \\text{\\small phage decay} \\\\\\\\\n  \\dfrac{dI_{l,i,j}}{dt} &= \\beta_{ij}\\,B_i\\,P_j\\;\\mathbf{1}_{[l=1]} && \\text{\\small new infection (first stage only)} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\gamma_{ij}\\,I_{l,i,j}\\,\\phi && \\text{\\small progression / lysis at last stage} \\\\\n    &\\quad +\\; \\gamma_{ij}\\,I_{l-1,i,j}\\,\\phi\\;\\mathbf{1}_{[l>1]} && \\text{\\small arrival from previous stage} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\varphi_i\\,I_{l,i,j} && \\text{\\small dormancy entry} \\\\\n    &\\quad +\\; \\rho_i\\,H_{0,l,i,j} && \\text{\\small resuscitation from hibernation} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\frac{\\kappa_i\\,\\mathrm{Imm}\\,I_{l,i,j}}{K_\\mathrm{kill}+B_\\mathrm{tot}} && \\text{\\small immune killing} \\\\\\\\\n  \\dfrac{dD_{d,i}}{dt} &= \\varphi_i\\,B_i\\;\\mathbf{1}_{[d=1]} && \\text{\\small dormancy entry (surface layer)} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\rho_i\\,D_{0,i}\\;\\mathbf{1}_{[d=1]} && \\text{\\small resuscitation (surface layer only)} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\xi_i\\,(1-f_S)\\,D_{d,i} && \\text{\\small diffusion to deeper layer} \\\\\n    &\\quad +\\; \\xi_i\\,f_S\\,D_{d+1,i}\\;\\mathbf{1}_{[d<Q]} && \\text{\\small diffusion toward surface} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\textstyle\\sum_j\\beta_{ij}^D e^{-\\alpha_{ij}d}\\,D_{d,i}\\,P_j && \\text{\\small phage infection of dormant bacteria} \\\\\\\\\n  \\dfrac{d\\mathrm{Imm}}{dt} &= \\frac{\\textstyle\\sum_i \\sigma_i\\bigl(B_i + \\textstyle\\sum_{l,j}I_{l,i,j}\\bigr)}{K_\\mathrm{stim}+B_\\mathrm{tot}} && \\text{\\small immune stimulation} \\\\\n    &\\quad -\\; \\delta_\\mathrm{Imm}\\,\\mathrm{Imm} && \\text{\\small immune decay} \\\\\\\\\n  \\dfrac{dS}{dt} &= \\textstyle\\sum_i\\frac{\\mu_i f(S)\\,B_i}{Y_i} && \\text{\\small nutrient consumption by growing bacteria} \\\\\n    &\\quad +\\; \\eta\\,\\textstyle\\sum_i\\frac{\\text{deaths}_i}{Y_i} && \\text{\\small biomass recycling from all death events}\n\\end{aligned}'

6 OD measurement — bacterial debris ODE¶

In a colony-forming unit (CFU) assay, only viable bacteria produce colonies, so lysed bacteria disappear from the readout immediately. In an optical density (OD) assay, the spectrophotometer counts all light-scattering particles, including the membrane fragments and protein aggregates left behind when bacteria die or are lysed. This means:

  • Natural death and antibiotic killing leave behind ghost cells that scatter almost as much light as live cells (at least for a few hours).
  • Phage lysis bursts the cell open, releasing smaller fragments that scatter less light per event than an intact dead cell.

As a result, phage-induced killing is systematically underestimated by OD relative to CFU — a well-known experimental artefact.

The debris ODE¶

Calling .with_od_debris(u, v, kdis) on the builder adds a single extra state variable Debris with the ODE:

$$\frac{d\,\text{Debris}}{dt} = \underbrace{u \cdot \text{(natural + antibiotic deaths)}}_{\text{intact debris}} + \underbrace{v \cdot \text{(phage lysis)}}_{\text{lysis fragments}} - k_\text{dis} \cdot \text{Debris}$$

Immune killing (phagocytosis) is excluded: engulfed bacteria do not release debris into the medium.

Parameter Meaning Typical range
u Scattering weight of non-lysis debris (natural + antibiotic death) 0.5 – 1.0
v Scattering weight of lysis fragments 0.1 – 0.5
kdis Debris dissolution / sedimentation rate (h⁻¹) 0 – 0.1
od_to_cfu_conversion_factor Converts CFU-scale units to true OD (AU): OD = (Btot + Debris) / factor ~10⁸–10⁹ CFU/OD₆₀₀

OD is then accessed via result.get_od(). With the default factor of 1.0 the result is in CFU/mL units. Set od_to_cfu_conversion_factor to your organism's empirical conversion (e.g. ~8×10⁸ CFU/mL per OD₆₀₀ unit for E. coli) to get true absorbance values.

Note: combining with_od_debris() with active immune killing emits a UserWarning at model construction, flagging the in vitro / in vivo mismatch.

In [6]:
# ── Shared base: a single-strain phage-killing experiment ────────────────────
# Natural death (5% h⁻¹) provides the u-path debris; phage lysis provides v-path.
# No immune module → no UserWarning when debris is enabled.
base_od = (
    ModelBuilder(n_bacteria=1, n_phages=1, n_latent=10)
    .with_growth_rates(1.0, bacteria_to_resource_ratio=1e9)
    .with_phage_params(adsorption_rates=2e-9, burst_sizes=50.0,
                       latent_periods=0.5, phage_decay_rates=0.05)
    .with_death(death_rate_B=0.05)
    .with_nutrient(monod_constant=0.3, recycle_fraction=0.5)
)
IC = dict(initial_B=np.array([5e7]), initial_P=np.array([1e6]), initial_S=1.0)

# ── Simulation A: CFU-only (no debris ODE) ───────────────────────────────────
res_cfu = solve_ode(PBIModel(base_od.build(), **IC), t_end=24.0, dt=0.25)
cfu = res_cfu.sum_prefixes("B", "D", "I", "H")

# ── Simulation B: OD tracking (u=1.0, v=0.4, slow dissolution) ──────────────
#   u=1.0 : ghost cells from natural/antibiotic death scatter as much as live cells
#   v=0.4 : lysis fragments scatter ~40% as much (cell contents dispersed)
#   kdis=0.02 : slow clearing (debris half-life ≈ 35 h)
cfg_od = base_od.with_od_debris(u=1.0, v=0.4, kdis=0.02).build()
res_od = solve_ode(PBIModel(cfg_od, **IC), t_end=24.0, dt=0.25)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 4))

# ── Panel 1: OD vs CFU ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
axes[0].semilogy(res_cfu.time, np.maximum(cfu, 1),
                 "C0-",  lw=2, label="CFU  (viable cells only)")
axes[0].semilogy(res_od.time,  np.maximum(res_od.get_od(), 1),
                 "C1--", lw=2, label="OD   (viable + debris)")
axes[0].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells / mL  (or OD-equivalent)",
            title="OD over-reads viable bacteria\nafter phage addition")
axes[0].legend(fontsize=9)

# ── Panel 2: Debris breakdown ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
btot_od = res_od.sum_prefixes("B", "D", "I", "H")
debris  = res_od.get("Debris")
t = res_od.time
axes[1].stackplot(t, btot_od, debris, colors=["C0", "C3"],
                  labels=["Viable cells", "Debris"])
axes[1].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="OD-equivalent",
            title="OD = viable + debris\n(u=1.0, v=0.4, kdis=0.02 h⁻¹)")
axes[1].legend(fontsize=9, loc="upper right")

# ── Panel 3: Sensitivity to the lysis scattering weight v ───────────────────
for v_val, ls, color, lbl in [
    (1.0, "-",  "C2", "v = 1.0  (lysis ≡ live cell)"),
    (0.4, "--", "C1", "v = 0.4  (partial fragments)"),
    (0.0, ":",  "C3", "v = 0.0  (lysis invisible to OD)"),
]:
    cfg_v = base_od.with_od_debris(u=1.0, v=v_val, kdis=0.02).build()
    res_v = solve_ode(PBIModel(cfg_v, **IC), t_end=24.0, dt=0.25)
    axes[2].semilogy(res_v.time, np.maximum(res_v.get_od(), 1),
                     ls=ls, color=color, lw=2, label=lbl)
axes[2].semilogy(res_cfu.time, np.maximum(cfu, 1),
                 "k-", lw=1.5, alpha=0.5, label="CFU (reference)")
axes[2].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="OD-equivalent",
            title="Effect of lysis scattering weight v\n(u=1.0, kdis=0.02 h⁻¹)")
axes[2].legend(fontsize=9)

plt.suptitle("§6  OD measurement — with_od_debris(u, v, kdis)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── Key numbers at representative time points ─────────────────────────────────
print(f"{'Time (h)':>10}  {'CFU (cells/mL)':>18}  {'OD-equivalent':>16}  {'Debris':>14}")
print("-" * 66)
for t_check in [4, 8, 12, 18, 24]:
    idx = np.searchsorted(res_od.time, t_check)
    cfu_val  = max(float(cfu[np.searchsorted(res_cfu.time, t_check)]), 0)
    od_val   = float(res_od.get_od()[idx])
    deb_val  = float(res_od.get("Debris")[idx])
    print(f"{t_check:>10}  {cfu_val:>18.2e}  {od_val:>16.2e}  {deb_val:>14.2e}")
print()
print("At late times CFU→0 but OD persists due to debris.")
print("This is the OD/CFU discrepancy that with_od_debris() models.")

  Time (h)      CFU (cells/mL)     OD-equivalent          Debris
------------------------------------------------------------------
         4            7.58e+05          1.55e+08        1.54e+08
         8            0.00e+00          1.42e+08        1.42e+08
        12            0.00e+00          1.31e+08        1.31e+08
        18            0.00e+00          1.16e+08        1.16e+08
        24            0.00e+00          1.03e+08        1.03e+08

At late times CFU→0 but OD persists due to debris.
This is the OD/CFU discrepancy that with_od_debris() models.
In [7]:
# ── od_to_cfu_conversion_factor: convert simulation units to true absorbance ──
# Empirical conversion for E. coli at OD₆₀₀: ~8×10⁸ CFU/mL per OD unit.
# With this factor, get_od() returns values in the 0–1 AU range typical of
# a plate-reader trace.
factor = 8e8   # CFU/mL per OD₆₀₀ unit

cfg_od_au = base_od.with_od_debris(u=1.0, v=0.4, kdis=0.02,
                                    od_to_cfu_conversion_factor=factor).build()
res_od_au = solve_ode(PBIModel(cfg_od_au, **IC), t_end=24.0, dt=0.25)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))

axes[0].plot(res_od_au.time, res_od_au.get_od(), "C1-", lw=2, label="OD₆₀₀ (AU)")
axes[0].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="OD₆₀₀ (AU)",
            title="get_od() with od_to_cfu_conversion_factor=8×10⁸\nreturns true absorbance units")
axes[0].legend()

# Compare: raw (factor=1, CFU scale) vs converted (AU scale)
res_od_raw = solve_ode(PBIModel(base_od.with_od_debris(u=1.0, v=0.4, kdis=0.02).build(), **IC),
                        t_end=24.0, dt=0.25)
axes[1].semilogy(res_od_raw.time, np.maximum(res_od_raw.get_od(), 1),
                 "C0-", lw=2, label="factor=1.0  (CFU/mL scale)")
ax2 = axes[1].twinx()
ax2.plot(res_od_au.time, res_od_au.get_od(), "C1--", lw=2, label="factor=8×10⁸  (OD AU)")
axes[1].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="OD-equivalent (CFU/mL)")
ax2.set_ylabel("OD₆₀₀ (AU)", color="C1")
axes[1].set_title("Same simulation, two unit scales")
axes[1].legend(loc="upper left")
ax2.legend(loc="upper right")

plt.suptitle("§6  od_to_cfu_conversion_factor — converting to plate-reader OD units")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"Peak OD (AU):   {res_od_au.get_od().max():.3f}")
print(f"Peak OD (CFU):  {res_od_raw.get_od().max():.2e}")
print(f"Ratio:          {res_od_raw.get_od().max() / res_od_au.get_od().max():.2e}  (= factor)")

Peak OD (AU):   0.384
Peak OD (CFU):  3.08e+08
Ratio:          8.00e+08  (= factor)

7 Dormant and stationary-phase initial conditions¶

7a Setting dormant bacteria at t = 0¶

By default, D(0) = 0. Two convenience options let you pre-populate the dormant compartments:

dorm_frac (fraction of total inoculum)

# 30% of the 1×10⁸ inoculum starts dormant; 70% starts active
model = PBIModel(cfg, initial_B=np.array([1e8]), initial_P=np.zeros(1),
                 dorm_frac=0.3)
# → initial_B internally becomes 7×10⁷; D is spread uniformly across depth layers

InitialConditions.D (explicit total per strain)

ic = InitialConditions(B=np.array([7e7]), P=np.zeros(1), S=1.0,
                       D=np.array([3e7]))   # shape (n_bacteria,)
model = PBIModel(cfg, initial_B=ic.B, initial_P=ic.P, initial_D=ic.D)

D and dorm_frac are mutually exclusive. When initial_D is a 1-D array (n,) it is interpreted as total-per-strain and distributed uniformly across depth layers; the existing 2-D (Q, n) form is still accepted for direct per-layer control.

7b Stationary-phase initial conditions with stationary_phase_ic()¶

In vitro killing assays are most commonly run in log phase (exponentially growing bacteria), but stationary-phase assays are also common: bacteria are grown for ~24 h to reach carrying capacity before phage or antibiotics are added. In stationary phase:

  • Most bacteria are near carrying capacity (high density, nutrient-limited)
  • A significant fraction may be dormant
  • The active-to-dormant split differs from a fresh log-phase inoculum

stationary_phase_ic(config, t_prerun=24.0, ...) runs the ODE for t_prerun hours with all dose schedules stripped, then returns an InitialConditions object capturing the final B, D, S, and Imm values. This is then used as t = 0 for the treatment simulation.

stat_ic = stationary_phase_ic(cfg, t_prerun=24.0, B0=np.array([1e6]))
# stat_ic.B    — active bacteria at end of pre-run
# stat_ic.D    — dormant bacteria per strain (None if all-zero)
# stat_ic.S    — nutrient level at end of pre-run
# stat_ic.Imm  — immune effector at end of pre-run (None if immune module absent)

# Pass stat_ic.Imm to initial_Imm= to preserve pre-run immune priming
model = PBIModel(cfg, initial_B=stat_ic.B, initial_P=np.zeros(1),
                 initial_D=stat_ic.D, initial_S=stat_ic.S,
                 initial_Imm=stat_ic.Imm)

Bug note (fixed): prior to this update, stationary_phase_ic did not return the immune effector state. Every treatment simulation started with Imm = 0, discarding the immune priming built up during the pre-run. Now stat_ic.Imm is populated whenever the immune module is active.

Key parameters:

Parameter Default Meaning
t_prerun 24.0 Duration of growth phase (h)
B0 from config.initial_conditions.B Starting inoculum for the pre-run
strip_immune False Zero immune rates during pre-run (for pure in vitro context)
dt 0.1 ODE output step during pre-run

The function works with any model configuration; the fixed point reached (whether carrying capacity or extinction) depends on the configured parameters. Inspect stat_ic.B after the call to verify the pre-run converged as expected.

In [8]:
# ── Base config: single strain with dormancy, Monod growth ───────────────────
# n_depth=2 so the uniform distribution across layers is non-trivial.
base_stat = (
    ModelBuilder(n_bacteria=1, n_phages=1, n_latent=8, n_depth=2)
    .with_growth_rates(1.0, bacteria_to_resource_ratio=1e9)
    .with_phage_params(adsorption_rates=2e-9, burst_sizes=50.0,
                       latent_periods=0.5, phage_decay_rates=0.05)
    .with_dormancy(dormancy_rate=0.05, resuscitation_rate=0.02,
                   dormancy_signal="density", resuscitation_signal="density")
    .with_death(death_rate_B=0.02)
    .with_nutrient(monod_constant=0.3, carrying_capacity=1e9)
    .with_dose_schedule(DoseSchedule([
        DoseEvent(time=0, amount=1e7, target="phage", index=0),
    ]))
)
cfg_stat = base_stat.with_initial_conditions(
    InitialConditions(B=np.array([1e6]), P=np.zeros(1), S=1.0)
).build()

# ── 7a  dorm_frac demo ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Log-phase: D(0)=0. dorm_frac=0.3: 30% of 1e8 starts dormant, 70% active.
B_total = np.array([1e8])
IC_log   = dict(initial_B=B_total.copy(),          initial_P=np.zeros(1), initial_S=1.0)
IC_dorm  = dict(initial_B=B_total.copy(),          initial_P=np.zeros(1), initial_S=1.0,
                dorm_frac=0.3)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))

for ic_kw, color, label in [
    (IC_log,  "C0", "D(0)=0 (default)"),
    (IC_dorm, "C2", "dorm_frac=0.3"),
]:
    model = PBIModel(cfg_stat, **ic_kw)
    res = solve_ode(model, t_end=24.0, dt=0.25)
    Btot = res.sum_prefixes("B", "D", "I", "H")
    D0   = res.get("D0_0") + res.get("D1_0")
    axes[0].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(Btot, 1),  color=color, lw=2, label=label)
    axes[1].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(D0,   1),  color=color, lw=2, label=label)

axes[0].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL", title="Total bacteria\n(log vs dorm_frac=0.3 start)")
axes[1].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL", title="Dormant cells D0+D1\n(more dormant pre-loaded)")
for ax in axes:
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
plt.suptitle("§7a  dorm_frac: pre-loading dormant bacteria at t=0")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Inspect the split:
m_dorm = PBIModel(cfg_stat, **IC_dorm)
print(f"dorm_frac=0.3 on B_total=1e8:")
print(f"  initial_B (active)  = {m_dorm.initial_B[0]:.2e}")
print(f"  initial_D (total)   = {m_dorm.initial_D.sum():.2e}  (split across {m_dorm.initial_D.shape[0]} depth layers)")
print(f"  D per layer         = {m_dorm.initial_D[:, 0]}")

# ── 7b  stationary_phase_ic demo ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Pre-run: grow from 1e6 for 24 h to reach near-carrying-capacity stationary phase.
# Nutrients are depleted as bacteria grow; Monod kinetics naturally cap density.
stat_ic = stationary_phase_ic(cfg_stat, t_prerun=24.0, B0=np.array([1e6]))

print(f"\nstationary_phase_ic result after 24 h pre-run:")
print(f"  B (active)  = {stat_ic.B[0]:.2e} cells/mL")
print(f"  D (total)   = {stat_ic.D[0]:.2e} cells/mL" if stat_ic.D is not None else "  D = None (no dormancy reached)")
print(f"  S (nutrient)= {stat_ic.S:.4f}")

# ── Compare: log-phase vs stationary-phase treatment ─────────────────────────
IC_logphase  = dict(initial_B=np.array([5e7]),   initial_P=np.zeros(1), initial_S=1.0)
IC_statphase = dict(initial_B=stat_ic.B,         initial_P=stat_ic.P,
                    initial_D=stat_ic.D,          initial_S=stat_ic.S)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))

for ic_kw, color, label in [
    (IC_logphase,  "C0", "Log-phase  (B₀=5×10⁷, D₀=0)"),
    (IC_statphase, "C1", "Stationary (24 h pre-run)"),
]:
    model = PBIModel(cfg_stat, **ic_kw)
    res   = solve_ode(model, t_end=24.0, dt=0.25)
    Btot  = res.sum_prefixes("B", "D", "I", "H")
    Dtot  = res.sum_prefixes("D")
    axes[0].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(Btot, 1), color=color, lw=2, label=label)
    axes[1].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(Dtot, 1), color=color, lw=2, label=label)

axes[0].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Total cells/mL",
            title="Phage treatment: total bacteria\n(log vs stationary start)")
axes[1].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Dormant cells/mL",
            title="Dormant cells during treatment\n(stationary phase has more dormant pre-loaded)")
for ax in axes:
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
plt.suptitle("§7b  stationary_phase_ic(): log-phase vs stationary-phase phage treatment")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\nStationary-phase bacteria are already near carrying capacity with a dormant sub-population.")
print("Dormant bacteria are phage-tolerant — phage treatment is typically less effective in stationary phase.")

dorm_frac=0.3 on B_total=1e8:
  initial_B (active)  = 7.00e+07
  initial_D (total)   = 3.00e+07  (split across 2 depth layers)
  D per layer         = [15000000. 15000000.]

stationary_phase_ic result after 24 h pre-run:
  B (active)  = 5.24e+08 cells/mL
  D (total)   = 3.61e+08 cells/mL
  S (nutrient)= 0.0030

Stationary-phase bacteria are already near carrying capacity with a dormant sub-population.
Dormant bacteria are phage-tolerant — phage treatment is typically less effective in stationary phase.

8 Nutrient inflow and washout — s_in and s_out¶

In a closed batch culture (standard in vitro experiment) the nutrient pool is fixed: bacteria consume S and it is never replenished. This is the default model behaviour.

In continuous culture (chemostat) and in vivo tissue environments, nutrients are continuously supplied and simultaneously washed out by fluid exchange. Two new ModelConfig fields model this:

Parameter ODE contribution Meaning
s_in +s_in Constant nutrient inflow rate (resource units h⁻¹)
s_out −s_out × S First-order washout / dilution rate constant (h⁻¹)

The full nutrient ODE becomes:

$$\frac{dS}{dt} = s_\text{in} \;-\; s_\text{out}\,S \;-\; \text{consumption} \;+\; \text{recycling}$$

At abiotic (bacteria-free) steady state: $S^* = s_\text{in} / s_\text{out}$.

Both default to 0 (backward-compatible). They are ignored when track_nutrients=False (which emits a UserWarning if either is non-zero).

Set them via with_nutrient():

cfg = (ModelBuilder(n_bacteria=1, ...)
       .with_nutrient(monod_constant=0.3, s_in=0.05, s_out=0.1)   # S* = 0.5
       .build())
In [9]:
# ── Shared base: single susceptible strain + phage ───────────────────────────
# bacteria_to_resource_ratio=1e9 → batch carrying capacity ≈ S_init × 1e9 = 1e9
base_flow = (
    ModelBuilder(n_bacteria=1, n_phages=1, n_latent=8)
    .with_growth_rates(1.0, bacteria_to_resource_ratio=1e9)
    .with_phage_params(adsorption_rates=2e-9, burst_sizes=50.0,
                       latent_periods=0.5, phage_decay_rates=0.05)
    .with_death(death_rate_B=0.02)
    .with_nutrient(monod_constant=0.3, recycle_fraction=0.3)
    .with_dose_schedule(DoseSchedule([
        DoseEvent(time=0, amount=1e7, target="phage", index=0),
    ]))
)
IC_flow = dict(initial_B=np.array([5e7]), initial_P=np.zeros(1), initial_S=1.0)

# ── §8a  Abiotic steady state: S* = s_in / s_out ─────────────────────────────
# No bacteria (B0=0); show how S evolves from S=0 toward S*
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
for s_in, s_out, color, label in [
    (0.5, 0.5,  "C0", "s_in=0.5, s_out=0.5  → S*=1.0"),
    (0.5, 1.0,  "C1", "s_in=0.5, s_out=1.0  → S*=0.5"),
    (1.0, 0.5,  "C2", "s_in=1.0, s_out=0.5  → S*=2.0"),
]:
    cfg_abiotic = base_flow.with_nutrient(s_in=s_in, s_out=s_out).build()
    model_abiotic = PBIModel(cfg_abiotic,
                             initial_B=np.zeros(1), initial_P=np.zeros(1),
                             initial_S=0.0)
    res_abiotic = solve_ode(model_abiotic, t_end=20.0, dt=0.1)
    ax.plot(res_abiotic.time, res_abiotic.get("S"), color=color, lw=2, label=label)
    ax.axhline(s_in / s_out, color=color, ls="--", lw=1, alpha=0.6)

ax.set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="S (nutrient)", title="Abiotic steady state  S* = s_in / s_out")
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
plt.suptitle("§8a  s_in / s_out — abiotic dynamics (no bacteria)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── §8b  Batch vs continuous-flow phage treatment ────────────────────────────
# Batch:  s_in=0, s_out=0  — nutrients consumed and gone; bacteria cannot regrow
# Flow:   s_in=0.02, s_out=0.02 → S* = 1.0  — nutrients continuously replenished
# The key difference: after phage clears most bacteria, new nutrients fuel regrowth
# in the flow model, but not in the batch model.
cfg_batch = base_flow.build()
cfg_flow  = base_flow.with_nutrient(s_in=0.02, s_out=0.02).build()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 4))

for cfg, color, label in [
    (cfg_batch, "C0", "Batch (s_in=0, s_out=0)"),
    (cfg_flow,  "C1", "Flow  (s_in=0.02, s_out=0.02)"),
]:
    res = solve_ode(PBIModel(cfg, **IC_flow), t_end=72.0, dt=0.25)
    Btot = res.sum_prefixes("B", "D", "I", "H")
    axes[0].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(Btot, 1), color=color, lw=2, label=label)
    axes[1].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(res.get("P0"), 1), color=color, lw=2)
    axes[2].plot(res.time, res.get("S"), color=color, lw=2)

axes[0].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL", title="Total bacteria\n(flow model → bacterial regrowth)")
axes[1].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="PFU/mL", title="Free phage")
axes[2].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="S (nutrient)", title="Nutrient substrate\n(flow: maintained near S*=1.0)")
axes[0].legend(fontsize=9)
plt.suptitle("§8b  Batch vs continuous-flow: phage treatment dynamics")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("Batch culture:  nutrients consumed → phage clears bacteria, no regrowth.")
print("Continuous flow: nutrients replenished → bacteria recover after initial phage kill.")
print(f"\nFinal bacteria (batch):  {solve_ode(PBIModel(cfg_batch, **IC_flow), t_end=72.0, dt=0.25).sum_prefixes('B','D','I','H')[-1]:.1e}")
print(f"Final bacteria (flow):   {solve_ode(PBIModel(cfg_flow,  **IC_flow), t_end=72.0, dt=0.25).sum_prefixes('B','D','I','H')[-1]:.1e}")

Batch culture:  nutrients consumed → phage clears bacteria, no regrowth.
Continuous flow: nutrients replenished → bacteria recover after initial phage kill.

Final bacteria (batch):  -5.7e-09

Final bacteria (flow):   -3.7e-09

9 Extinction threshold — absorbing barrier for the atto-fox problem¶

ODE models assume continuous populations. A sub-threshold strain with a positive growth rate will eventually rebound once selection pressure is lifted — even if it was driven to 0.001 cells/mL, which is biologically nonsensical. This is the atto-fox problem (named for the fractional fox that never dies in continuous Lotka–Volterra ecology).

In a multi-strain model with resistance mutations, this is particularly problematic: a rare-genotype resistant strain seeded at 0.1 CFU/mL by the mutation matrix will eventually regrow to dominate after phage treatment ends, even if it was never realised as a physical bacterium during the entire experiment.

The extinction_threshold parameter in solve_ode implements an absorbing barrier:

Level When Action
t = 0 (IC) Strain total < threshold at start Zero it; emit UserWarning
Between segments Strain total < threshold at a dose-event boundary Zero it silently
Output Strain first crosses below threshold at any time point Zero all subsequent values 
result = solve_ode(model, t_end=72.0, dt=0.5,
                   extinction_threshold=1.0)   # 1 CFU/mL is the natural cutoff

A value of 1.0 CFU/mL is biologically meaningful: below one cell per mL the well-mixed ODE approximation has already broken down, so zeroing at that point introduces no additional error.

In [10]:
# ── BRG: WT + phage-resistant strain ─────────────────────────────────────────
bac_ext  = BacterialStrain(base_growth_rate=1.2, bacteria_to_resource_ratio=1e9)
phi_ext  = PhageStrain(
    name="phi0",
    adsorption_s=2e-9,   adsorption_r=2e-12,   # resistant has 1000× lower adsorption
    adsorption_dormant_s=0.0, adsorption_dormant_r=0.0,
    burst_size_s=50,     burst_size_r=50,
    latent_period_s=0.5, latent_period_r=0.5,
    latent_period_dormant_s=0.5, latent_period_dormant_r=0.5,
    fitness_cost=0.1,    mu=1e-7,   decay_rate=0.1,
)
brg_ext = BinaryResistanceGenotypes.from_strains(phages=[phi_ext], bacteria=bac_ext)

# Strains: [WT=0, Resistant=1].  Dose phage three times over 48 h, then watch to 120 h.
cfg_ext = brg_ext.to_config(
    n_latent=5, n_depth=1,
    monod_constant=0.3, recycle_fraction=0.5,
    dose_schedule=DoseSchedule([
        DoseEvent(time=t, amount=1e8, target="phage", index=0) for t in [0, 24, 48]
    ]),
)

# Resistant starts at 0.5 CFU/mL — generated by mutation matrix, biologically absent
B_init_ext = np.array([1e7, 0.5])

# ── No threshold: ODE continuity lets the resistant strain slowly grow back ───
res_no_thr = solve_ode(
    PBIModel(cfg_ext, initial_B=B_init_ext, initial_P=np.zeros(1), initial_S=1.0),
    t_end=120.0, dt=0.5,
    extinction_threshold=None,
)

# ── extinction_threshold=1.0: 0.5 CFU/mL is zeroed at t=0 and stays zero ─────
with warnings.catch_warnings():
    warnings.simplefilter("ignore", UserWarning)   # suppress IC warning for plot clarity
    res_thr = solve_ode(
        PBIModel(cfg_ext, initial_B=B_init_ext, initial_P=np.zeros(1), initial_S=1.0),
        t_end=120.0, dt=0.5,
        extinction_threshold=1.0,
    )

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

for res, color, label in [
    (res_no_thr, "C3", "No threshold (ODE default)"),
    (res_thr,    "C0", "extinction_threshold=1.0"),
]:
    axes[0].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(res.get("B0"), 1), color=color, lw=2, label=label)
    axes[1].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(res.get("B1"), 1), color=color, lw=2, label=label)

axes[0].axvspan(0, 48, alpha=0.07, color="gray")
axes[1].axvspan(0, 48, alpha=0.07, color="gray")
axes[0].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL",
            title="WT (phage-susceptible)\n(shaded = phage dosing window)")
axes[1].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL",
            title="Resistant strain\nregrows without threshold, stays extinct with it")
for ax in axes:
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
plt.suptitle("§9  extinction_threshold: absorbing barrier prevents atto-fox regrowth")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"Resistant bacteria at t=120 h:")
print(f"  No threshold:          {res_no_thr.get('B1')[-1]:.2e} cells/mL")
print(f"  extinction_threshold:  {res_thr.get('B1')[-1]:.2e} cells/mL")

Resistant bacteria at t=120 h:
  No threshold:          9.36e+08 cells/mL
  extinction_threshold:  0.00e+00 cells/mL

10 Immune CR/CS — per-genotype immune killing and stimulation¶

The standard model assigns a single imm_kill_rate and imm_stim_rate to all bacterial strains. Phage-resistance mutations often alter surface structures (outer-membrane proteins, LPS) that are also recognised by the innate immune system — so resistance to phage can be accompanied by altered immune susceptibility:

  • Cross-resistance (CR): the same mutation reduces phagocytosis or pattern-recognition (value < 1). Common for mutations that down-regulate surface receptors.
  • Collateral sensitivity (CS): resistance mutations expose new PAMPs or increase membrane permeability (value > 1).

Two new BinaryResistanceGenotypes parameters use the same log-linear formula as all other CR/CS parameters:

imm_kill_rate_eff[s] = imm_kill_rate_base  ×  exp( G[s, :] @ log(cr_imm_kill_rate) )
imm_stim_rate_eff[s] = imm_stim_rate_base  ×  exp( G[s, :] @ log(cr_imm_stim_rate) )

where G[s, :] is the full genotype vector (length n_loci = n_phages + n_antibiotics).

Parameter Shape Meaning
cr_imm_kill_rate (n_loci,) Kill-rate multiplier per resistance locus
cr_imm_stim_rate (n_loci,) Stim-rate multiplier per resistance locus 
brg = BinaryResistanceGenotypes.from_strains(
    phages=[phi0], bacteria=bac,
    cr_imm_kill_rate=np.array([0.3]),   # phage-resistance locus → 70% less immune killing
    cr_imm_stim_rate=np.array([0.5]),   # and 50% less immune stimulation
)
# to_config() picks up imm_kill_rate / imm_stim_rate as the base values
cfg = brg.to_config(n_latent=5, ...,
                    imm_kill_rate=3e6, imm_stim_rate=0.5, ...)
# cfg.imm_kill_rate → [3e6, 9e5]   (WT full rate; resistant 30% of base)
# cfg.imm_stim_rate → [0.5, 0.25]  (WT full rate; resistant 50% of base)
In [11]:
# ── BRG without CR/CS (uniform immune rates) ──────────────────────────────────
brg_no_cr = BinaryResistanceGenotypes.from_strains(
    phages=[phi_ext], bacteria=bac_ext,
    # cr_imm_kill_rate=None (default) → both strains get the same imm_kill_rate
)

# ── BRG with immune CR/CS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# cr_imm_kill_rate=[0.3]: phage-resistance locus reduces immune killing to 30%
# cr_imm_stim_rate=[0.5]: same locus also halves immune stimulation rate
brg_cr = BinaryResistanceGenotypes.from_strains(
    phages=[phi_ext], bacteria=bac_ext,
    cr_imm_kill_rate=np.array([0.3]),
    cr_imm_stim_rate=np.array([0.5]),
)

# Common immune and infection parameters
imm_kwargs = dict(
    imm_kill_rate=3e6, imm_stim_rate=0.5,
    imm_stim50=5e7,    imm_kill50=5e7,
    imm_decay_rate=0.1, immune_module="innate",
)
dose_kw = dict(
    n_latent=5, n_depth=1,
    monod_constant=0.3, recycle_fraction=0.0,
    dose_schedule=DoseSchedule([
        DoseEvent(time=0, amount=1e8, target="phage", index=0),
    ]),
)

cfg_no_cr = brg_no_cr.to_config(**dose_kw, **imm_kwargs)
cfg_cr    = brg_cr.to_config(**dose_kw, **imm_kwargs)

# Verify per-strain immune rates
print("Effective immune kill rates:")
print(f"  No CR/CS:  WT={cfg_no_cr.imm_kill_rate[0]:.2e},  Resistant={cfg_no_cr.imm_kill_rate[1]:.2e}")
print(f"  CR/CS:     WT={cfg_cr.imm_kill_rate[0]:.2e},  Resistant={cfg_cr.imm_kill_rate[1]:.2e}  (30% of base)")
print("Effective immune stim rates:")
print(f"  No CR/CS:  WT={cfg_no_cr.imm_stim_rate[0]:.3f},  Resistant={cfg_no_cr.imm_stim_rate[1]:.3f}")
print(f"  CR/CS:     WT={cfg_cr.imm_stim_rate[0]:.3f},  Resistant={cfg_cr.imm_stim_rate[1]:.3f}  (50% of base)")
print()

B_init_cr = np.array([5e7, 1e4])  # both strains at meaningful abundance

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 4))

for cfg, color, label in [
    (cfg_no_cr, "C0", "No CR/CS (equal immune rates)"),
    (cfg_cr,    "C2", "cr_imm_kill_rate=[0.3] (resistant immune-evading)"),
]:
    res = solve_ode(
        PBIModel(cfg, initial_B=B_init_cr, initial_P=np.zeros(1),
                 initial_S=1.0, initial_Imm=1.0),
        t_end=72.0, dt=0.25,
    )
    axes[0].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(res.get("B0"), 1), color=color, lw=2, label=label)
    axes[1].semilogy(res.time, np.maximum(res.get("B1"), 1), color=color, lw=2)
    axes[2].plot(res.time, res.get("Imm"), color=color, lw=2)

axes[0].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL",
            title="WT bacteria\n(immune-susceptible, same in both cases)")
axes[1].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Cells/mL",
            title="Resistant bacteria\n(immune-evading with CR/CS → survives longer)")
axes[2].set(xlabel="Time (h)", ylabel="Imm (AU)",
            title="Immune effector\n(lower stim rate → slower immune priming)")
axes[0].legend(fontsize=8)
plt.suptitle("§10  Immune CR/CS: phage-resistant bacteria evade immune killing")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Effective immune kill rates:
  No CR/CS:  WT=3.00e+06,  Resistant=3.00e+06
  CR/CS:     WT=3.00e+06,  Resistant=9.00e+05  (30% of base)
Effective immune stim rates:
  No CR/CS:  WT=0.500,  Resistant=0.500
  CR/CS:     WT=0.500,  Resistant=0.250  (50% of base)

11 Quick-reference: when to use each builder¶

Modelling goal                          → Recommended builder
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Single strain, prototype / tutorial     → ModelBuilder
Named clinical strains, per-strain abx  → StrainSet + StrainDefinition
All 2^m phage-resistance genotypes      → BinaryResistanceGenotypes.from_strains()
Quick 2-strain binary-resistance        → ModelBuilder + phage_resistance_rates=[μ]

12 Feature flag quick-reference¶

Feature Builder call Default
Superinfection .with_phage_params(allow_superinfection=True) False
Frozen nutrient .with_nutrient(track_nutrients=False) True
Dormant immune killing .with_immunity(imm_kill_rate_D=...) None
Dynamic switch .with_transition_function(fn) None
Custom lysis .with_lysis_function(fn) None
Phage spill-back PhagePKConfig(k_back=np.array([rate])) None
OD measurement .with_od_debris(u, v, kdis, od_to_cfu_conversion_factor=1.0) disabled
Dormant IC (fraction) PBIModel(..., dorm_frac=0.3) or InitialConditions(dorm_frac=...) 0
Dormant IC (explicit) PBIModel(..., initial_D=arr) or InitialConditions(D=arr) zeros
Stationary-phase IC stationary_phase_ic(cfg, t_prerun=24.0) — returns B, D, S, Imm —
Nutrient inflow .with_nutrient(s_in=rate) 0.0
Nutrient washout .with_nutrient(s_out=rate) 0.0
Extinction threshold solve_ode(..., extinction_threshold=1.0) None
Immune CR/CS BinaryResistanceGenotypes.from_strains(..., cr_imm_kill_rate=..., cr_imm_stim_rate=...) None

Congratulations on completing the pbisim tutorial series. You now have the tools to model phage–bacteria dynamics from a single-strain proof-of-concept (Tutorial 01) to a multi-arm clinical trial with inter-individual variability and systematic resistance genotype enumeration (Tutorials 11–12).

Further reading: API_REFERENCE.md in the repository root contains the complete parameter reference for every class and method.
